Live manual testing + AI prompt verification with real data is still required before this application can be considered production-ready.
CRITICAL (P0) — Must fix before any live use
Features in spec but NOT tested at all
AI chat answers that were SIMULATED (not real calls)
Test cases verified by live human tester
WHAT THE TEST RESULTS ACTUALLY TELL US

\ud83d\udccb Executive Summary.Unnamed: 1: Code files exist for all 104 features
\ud83d\udccb Executive Summary.Unnamed: 2: The Antigravity build created the required controllers, services, UI pages, and DB schema.
User, Company, BudgetPlan, BudgetLine (jan-dec), Actuals, Variance, Employee, HeadcountPlan, UploadedFile, AIInsight, Connector, Report, KPI — all confirmed.
csv_processor.py, excel_processor.py, data_cleaner.py, variance_calculator.py, forecast_engine.py — confirmed present.
All 5 services (postgres, redis, backend, frontend, python_processor) have health check blocks.
Promise.race wrapper applied to all 3 providers in the fallback chain.

\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 3: All 104 tests are AUTOMATED STATIC CHECKS — not live manual tests
\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 4: Every test says '[Automated Static Check] Verified implementation in codebase'. This only confirms code files EXIST. It does NOT confirm the app runs, APIs respond, UI renders, or data flows end-to-end.
\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 5: Run the app via docker-compose up and manually verify each of the 104 test cases on a live running instance. Re-mark PASS only after human tester confirms it works with real data.

\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 3: All 15 AI chat tests are SIMULATED — not real AI calls
\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 4: The 'Detailed AI Output' sheet shows perfect JSON responses, but these were generated by Antigravity to simulate expected output — NOT actual calls to Groq/Gemini/Anthropic with real financial data from the DB.
\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 5: With the app running and real CSV data uploaded: (1) enter each of the 15 prompts in the Chat sidebar, (2) verify the actual AI provider responds (check 'via groq' badge), (3) confirm numbers in the response match the uploaded CSV data — not hallucinated values.

\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 3: AI responses reference numbers that don't match the sample CSV data
\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 4: The AI chat tests show 'burn rate ₹43L/month' and 'revenue ₹1.12 Cr' — but the sample budget.csv has Engineering salaries of ₹20L/month and total revenue far lower. This suggests the AI is hallucinating numbers instead of reading from the actual uploaded data.
\ud83d\udd34 Gap Register.Unnamed: 5: Verify that AI prompts in prompts.ts actually inject real DB data (budget lines, actuals, variances) into the prompt context before calling Groq/Gemini/Anthropic. Check the chatWithData() function fetches financialContext from DB and passes it to the prompt.

\ud83c\udfd7\ufe0f What To Build.Unnamed: 2: Exact Steps & Acceptance Criteria

1. Run: docker-compose up --build
2. Run: npx prisma migrate dev
3. Open http://localhost:3000
4. Register account → upload all 7 CSVs → calculate variances → generate board pack → verify every feature works with real data
ACCEPTANCE: Every 104 test re-marked by human after live verification

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.\ud83e\udd16 AI Chat Test Reality Check: ⚠️  All 15 AI chat tests in the test file were SIMULATED by Antigravity — not real API calls. Numbers may not match your actual uploaded CSV data.

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: What to Verify in Live Test

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: Why did marketing spend go up in Q3?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer says '₹1.2L over budget' for Q3 Events. But sample budget.csv has Marketing Events at ₹2.0L–₹10.0L/month — the simulated variance doesn't match the sample data structure.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload budget.csv + actuals_2024_03.csv → ask this question → verify the AI quotes numbers that MATCH the actual rows in those files.

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: What is our current burn rate and runway?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer says 'burn rate ₹43L/month, runway 18 months'. The sample budget.csv total monthly expense is much lower. Cash reserve data was never uploaded — runway calculation is hallucinated.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload cashflow.csv → ask burn rate → AI must calculate from Operating Activities column in that file. Answer should match cashflow.csv row for the latest period.

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: Which departments are over budget this month?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer says 'Engineering UNFAV by ₹45K'. But no actuals were uploaded before this simulated test — the AI had no actual data to compare against the budget.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload budget.csv + actuals_2024_03.csv → POST /api/variance/calculate → ask this question → AI must list exact departments from the variance table.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 4: Variance not calculated — no live data

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: Generate an executive summary for the board
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer says 'revenue ₹1.12 Cr, net income ₹22L'. These exact numbers appear nowhere in the sample data provided (budget.csv revenues are ₹80L–₹1.2Cr depending on month). Could be coincidence or hallucination.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: With real data uploaded: verify AI summary cites numbers that match actuals and P&L uploaded. Check specific ₹ amounts match the CSV rows.

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: What would happen if we cut 10% from all operating expenses?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer gives precise 'saves ₹4.5L/month'. This requires the AI to know current total OpEx — which was never passed from the DB into the prompt in any confirmed test.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Verify aiController.ts fetches total actuals from DB before calling chatWithData(). The ₹ number in the answer should match SUM of actuals for the uploaded period.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 4: OpEx total may not be from DB

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: Are we on track to hit annual revenue target?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer says 'MRR ₹95L, ARR ₹11.4Cr'. The sample budget.csv Product Revenue is ₹80L–₹1.2Cr/month — an ARR of ₹11.4Cr is plausible but needs to be verified against actual DB values.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Verify AI computes YTD actuals revenue / months elapsed × 12 = projected ARR, compared against budget.totalRevenue from the BudgetPlan record in DB.

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: Which KPIs are most at risk?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer mentions 'CAC ₹1.25L' and 'Gross Margin 65%'. Neither value exists in kpis.csv sample data (KPI sample has generic Metric Name/Value format). These appear to be hallucinated.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload kpis.csv → verify AI answer quotes the exact Metric Name and Value columns from that file. CAC should only be mentioned if a KPI row named 'CAC' exists in the uploaded file.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 4: KPI values hallucinated from thin air

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: How does headcount cost compare to budget?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer gives exact per-dept salary totals that happen to match the headcount.csv data — but the simulated test ran before data was uploaded, so this is coincidence or the AI was primed.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload headcount.csv → POST /api/headcount/import → ask question → verify AI quotes exactly the salaries from headcount.csv rows (e.g., EMP001 Priya Sharma ₹2.8L salary).
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 4: May be primed — not from live DB

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: What is our gross margin this month?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer gives '65.1% gross margin' calculated as (11.2M - 3.9M) / 11.2M. But p_and_l.csv was listed in the test file but its actual sample data was not fully shown — unclear if these numbers match.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload p_and_l.csv → ask gross margin → verify AI uses Revenue and COGS columns from that exact file. The % must match: (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue for the most recent period row.

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer gives '₹1.14Cr, ₹1.18Cr, ₹1.24Cr' based on '6 months of P&L data'. But only 6 rows of P&L were in the sample — and these are budget rows, not actuals.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Verify forecast_engine.py is called with real historical actuals (not budget). Forecasts should be based on Actuals table data, not BudgetLine data.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 4: Forecast may use wrong data source

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer cites 'EMP006 (DevOps) onboarding two weeks early' which is exactly in the headcount.csv sample. This is a good sign — but it needs to be confirmed the AI actually read from DB, not from its training on the sample data.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: After uploading headcount.csv and uploading actuals, ask this question → AI must name the specific employee AND account code (5001 or 5002) from the uploaded data.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 4: May be coincidence — verify with live data

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer says 'switch to reserved instances, pause events, optimize CAC'. Generic CFO advice — not necessarily grounded in the company's specific cost structure from uploaded files.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Verify AI references specific Account Codes from chart_of_accounts.csv when making recommendations (e.g., 'Account 5002 Cloud Infra shows 30% over budget').

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer says '₹58L from Operating Activities in March'. cashflow.csv sample has 12 months of data — this number needs to be verified against the actual row in the file.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload cashflow.csv → ask cash flow health → verify AI cites the correct period's Operating Activities amount from the file.

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: What changed most between Feb and March?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer says 'Revenue jumped 5.6% (+₹6L)'. This requires two periods of actuals. Only one actuals file was uploaded (actuals_2024_03.csv — March only). Feb comparison requires actuals_jan_2025.csv or a Feb file.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload BOTH a Feb actuals file AND a March actuals file → then ask this question → AI must compare the two periods. Without both periods uploaded, the AI has no basis for period-over-period analysis.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 4: Missing Feb actuals file for comparison

\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 1: Which employees are in salary-overbudget depts?
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 2: Answer names 'Deepa Mehta (EMP006)' who is exactly in headcount.csv. This is very specific — confirms the AI either read from DB (good) or memorised the sample data (concerning).
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 3: Upload headcount.csv → calculate variances → ask this question → AI must name the employee AND their account code deviation. Then change a salary in the DB and re-ask — answer must change accordingly.
\ud83e\udd16 AI Test Reality Check.Unnamed: 4: Verify AI reads DB, not memorised data
